Luis López.bio: Experienced Honduran goalkeeper who has been a mainstay for Real Espana in the Liga Nacional. A reliable presence between the posts who has represented Honduras at multiple levels.
Luis López.playingStyle: Commanding goalkeeper with good positioning and strong shot-stopping ability, vocal organizer of the defense
Luis López.worldCup2026Role: Starting goalkeeper for Honduras in their World Cup campaign
Luis López.notableFact: Has been one of the top goalkeepers in Honduras' domestic league for several seasons with Real Espana

Andy Najar.bio: Veteran right-back who made history as the first Honduran to play in the Belgian Pro League with Anderlecht before returning to MLS with DC United. One of the most experienced players in the squad with 55 caps.
Andy Najar.playingStyle: Technically skilled full-back with experience at the highest European level, capable of contributing in attack and defense
Andy Najar.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back bringing European experience and offensive quality
Andy Najar.notableFact: First Honduran to play in Belgium's top division, spending several seasons with RSC Anderlecht

Jose García.bio: Young center-back who has established himself in MLS with Orlando City. Part of the new generation of Honduran defenders stepping up to replace the legendary defensive stalwarts.
Jose García.playingStyle: Athletic, modern center-back comfortable on the ball and strong in aerial duels
Jose García.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back representing the next generation of Honduran defenders
Jose García.notableFact: One of the youngest defenders in the squad, earning MLS experience with Orlando City

Wesly Decas.bio: Domestic league center-back playing for CD Marathon in the Liga Nacional de Honduras. A solid defender who has earned his place through consistent performances at club level.
Wesly Decas.playingStyle: Tough, physical center-back who excels in defensive duels and brings intensity to every challenge
Wesly Decas.worldCup2026Role: Defensive depth option providing cover at center-back from the domestic league
Wesly Decas.notableFact: A product of Honduras' competitive domestic league who has risen to earn international recognition

Denil Maldonado.bio: Center-back who has achieved the dream of playing in Liga MX with Pachuca, one of the most experienced defenders in the squad with 35 caps. A physically imposing presence at the heart of the defense.
Denil Maldonado.playingStyle: Strong, commanding center-back who dominates in the air and is aggressive in ground duels
Denil Maldonado.worldCup2026Role: Key center-back and defensive leader, likely to start alongside Garcia
Denil Maldonado.notableFact: One of the few Honduran players currently playing in Mexico's prestigious Liga MX with Pachuca

Bryan Acosta.bio: One of Honduras' most experienced midfielders with 65 caps and 3 goals. Has spent years in MLS with FC Dallas, serving as a vital cog in the midfield engine for both club and country.
Bryan Acosta.playingStyle: Tenacious, combative midfielder who wins the ball and keeps play moving with efficient distribution
Bryan Acosta.worldCup2026Role: Experienced midfield anchor providing leadership and defensive stability in the center
Bryan Acosta.notableFact: Has been one of Honduras' most consistent performers in MLS, accumulating over 65 international caps

Jorge Álvarez.bio: Exciting young winger playing for New York City FC in MLS. A quick, skillful attacker who represents the exciting future of Honduran football on the wing.
Jorge Álvarez.playingStyle: Pacy, direct winger who uses his speed and close control to beat defenders and create chances from wide areas
Jorge Álvarez.worldCup2026Role: Attacking option on the right wing providing pace and creativity
Jorge Álvarez.notableFact: Playing in New York with NYCFC has given him exposure to a high-profile MLS environment

Carlos Pineda.bio: Central midfielder who captains Olympia in the Liga Nacional de Honduras, one of the country's most storied clubs. A technically gifted player who sets the tempo for club and country.
Carlos Pineda.playingStyle: Technical, intelligent midfielder who controls the game's rhythm with his passing and positional awareness
Carlos Pineda.worldCup2026Role: Midfield option providing technical quality and an ability to control possession
Carlos Pineda.notableFact: Plays for Olimpia, Honduras' most successful and decorated football club

Anthony Lozano.bio: Honduras' most dangerous striker with 20 goals in 65 caps, Lozano has represented his country at the highest level. Has experience in La Liga with Cadiz and Barcelona B, making him the most seasoned attacker in the squad.
Anthony Lozano.playingStyle: Clinical finisher with intelligent movement who can score from various positions and is effective both through the middle and from the right
Anthony Lozano.worldCup2026Role: First-choice striker and leading goalscorer, Honduras' primary goal threat
Anthony Lozano.notableFact: Scored 20 goals for Honduras and has experience in Spanish football including time with Barcelona's B team

Kervin Arriaga.bio: Dynamic central midfielder playing in MLS for Minnesota United. Known for his box-to-box abilities and set-piece delivery, he has quickly become an important player for Honduras.
Kervin Arriaga.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder with a powerful engine who contributes goals from midfield and delivers dangerous set pieces
Kervin Arriaga.worldCup2026Role: Key midfielder providing energy, goals, and set-piece quality in the center of the park
Kervin Arriaga.notableFact: His set-piece delivery has been a significant weapon for Honduras in qualifying matches

Romell Quioto.bio: Experienced winger with 50 caps and 12 goals who has been a consistent performer in MLS for CF Montreal. A direct, powerful attacker who has been one of Honduras' most reliable wide players for years.
Romell Quioto.playingStyle: Powerful, direct winger who uses his physicality and pace to drive past defenders and deliver end product
Romell Quioto.worldCup2026Role: Starting left winger providing width, directness, and a significant goal threat
Romell Quioto.notableFact: Has scored over 12 international goals and been a consistent performer across multiple CONCACAF campaigns

Harold Fonseca.bio: Backup goalkeeper playing for Marathon in the Liga Nacional de Honduras. A reliable domestic league keeper who provides important depth in the goalkeeping department.
Harold Fonseca.playingStyle: Solid, dependable goalkeeper with good handling and a calm presence in goal
Harold Fonseca.notableFact: Has been a consistent performer in Honduras' domestic league with Marathon for several seasons

Joseph Rosales.bio: Young midfielder who has made a name for himself in MLS with Houston Dynamo. An energetic player who brings dynamism and creativity to the Honduran midfield.
Joseph Rosales.playingStyle: Creative, energetic midfielder who combines technical skill with the ability to cover significant ground
Joseph Rosales.worldCup2026Role: Midfield rotation option bringing creativity and youthful energy
Joseph Rosales.notableFact: His performances for Houston Dynamo in MLS earned him a regular spot in the Honduras national team

Diego Rodríguez.bio: Center-back playing for Motagua in the Liga Nacional de Honduras, one of the country's biggest clubs. A solid domestic defender who adds depth to the squad.
Diego Rodríguez.playingStyle: Reliable, organized center-back who excels in positional play and aerial challenges
Diego Rodríguez.worldCup2026Role: Defensive depth option providing cover from Honduras' domestic league
Diego Rodríguez.notableFact: Plays for Motagua, one of the most successful and popular clubs in Honduran football

Jonathan Rubio.bio: Winger who has gained European experience playing in Portugal's second division with Academica. Brings a different tactical perspective from his time in European football.
Jonathan Rubio.playingStyle: Skillful winger with good close control and the ability to create chances from wide positions
Jonathan Rubio.worldCup2026Role: Wide attacking option bringing European football experience to the squad
Jonathan Rubio.notableFact: One of the few Honduran players currently playing in European football, based in Portugal

Deybi Flores.bio: Central midfielder who has been a key player for Motagua in the Honduran domestic league. A consistent performer who brings stability and reliability to the midfield.
Deybi Flores.playingStyle: Disciplined, intelligent midfielder who excels at keeping possession and recycling the ball efficiently
Deybi Flores.worldCup2026Role: Midfield depth option providing stability and experience from domestic football
Deybi Flores.notableFact: A stalwart for Motagua who has been instrumental in their domestic league campaigns

Alberth Elis.bio: One of Honduras' most talented and valuable attackers who has played in MLS, Ligue 1, and the Premier League with Bordeaux and Boavista. A pacy winger with an eye for goal, though currently without a club.
Alberth Elis.playingStyle: Lightning-fast winger with explosive acceleration, direct dribbling, and a strong goal-scoring instinct
Alberth Elis.worldCup2026Role: Key attacking weapon expected to be one of Honduras' most dangerous players when fit
Alberth Elis.notableFact: Honduras' most valuable attacker with experience across MLS, French, Portuguese, and Turkish football

Brayan Moya.bio: Versatile forward who has scored 8 goals in 30 caps for Honduras while playing in Bolivia with Club Bolivar. A reliable goal threat who contributes both goals and assists.
Brayan Moya.playingStyle: Intelligent forward with good link-up play and the ability to operate as both a striker and an attacking midfielder
Brayan Moya.worldCup2026Role: Important attacking option who can play centrally or out wide, providing tactical flexibility
Brayan Moya.notableFact: Has been one of the top scorers in the Bolivian Primera Division while maintaining his Honduras career

Edwin Rodríguez.bio: One of the most exciting young prospects in Honduran football, playing for Bayern Munich II in Germany's Regionalliga. His development at one of the world's biggest clubs highlights his immense potential.
Edwin Rodríguez.playingStyle: Technically gifted young midfielder with excellent passing ability and the tactical discipline developed in German football
Edwin Rodríguez.worldCup2026Role: Exciting young prospect who could be a breakout star on the World Cup stage
Edwin Rodríguez.notableFact: Training and playing within the Bayern Munich system, one of the most prestigious development environments in world football

Jerry Bengtson.bio: Honduras' most experienced and prolific striker with an incredible 25 goals in 70 caps. At 39, the Olimpia legend is the elder statesman of the squad and a national hero.
Jerry Bengtson.playingStyle: Tall, powerful target man who excels in the air and uses his experience and positioning to score goals
Jerry Bengtson.worldCup2026Role: Veteran squad leader whose experience and leadership are invaluable, potential impact substitute
Jerry Bengtson.notableFact: Honduras' top active goalscorer with 25 goals, a legend of the national team and Olimpia

Marlon Licona.bio: Third-choice goalkeeper who plays for Olimpia in the Liga Nacional de Honduras. Provides additional depth in the goalkeeping department.
Marlon Licona.playingStyle: Athletic goalkeeper with good reflexes and the desire to command his penalty area
Marlon Licona.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper providing squad depth and emergency cover
Marlon Licona.notableFact: Plays for Olimpia, the most successful club in Honduran football history

Jonathan Trigueros.bio: Left-back from Motagua who has been developing in the Honduran domestic league. A left-footed defender who provides balance and width on the left side of defense.
Jonathan Trigueros.playingStyle: Attacking left-back who uses his left foot to deliver crosses and supports the winger ahead of him
Jonathan Trigueros.worldCup2026Role: Starting or backup left-back providing a natural left-footed option in defense
Jonathan Trigueros.notableFact: One of the few natural left-footed defenders in the Honduran squad, playing for domestic giants Motagua

Rigoberto Rivas.bio: Honduran-born winger who grew up in Italy and came through Inter Milan's youth system before moving to Turkey. A technically polished player with a European football education.
Rigoberto Rivas.playingStyle: Skillful, left-footed winger who can play on either flank, using his technical quality and creativity to unlock defenses
Rigoberto Rivas.worldCup2026Role: Creative attacking option on the wing, bringing European-trained technical quality
Rigoberto Rivas.notableFact: Developed in Inter Milan's prestigious youth academy before pursuing his senior career in Italian and Turkish football

Cristian Cálix.bio: Young attacking midfielder from Motagua who is considered one of Honduras' brightest young talents. A creative spark who can unlock defenses with his vision and passing.
Cristian Cálix.playingStyle: Creative playmaker with quick feet, excellent vision, and the ability to thread passes through tight spaces
Cristian Cálix.worldCup2026Role: Young creative option who can change games from the bench with his flair and invention
Cristian Cálix.notableFact: Regarded as one of the most promising young talents to emerge from Honduran domestic football in recent years

Isaac Castillo.bio: Young right winger playing for Real Espana in the Liga Nacional de Honduras. A pacy, direct attacker who has been developing rapidly in domestic competition.
Isaac Castillo.playingStyle: Quick, direct winger who uses his speed to stretch defenses and create chances from wide positions
Isaac Castillo.worldCup2026Role: Young wide attacker providing fresh legs and pace as a substitute option
Isaac Castillo.notableFact: Has been one of the standout young attackers in Honduras' domestic league with Real Espana

Luis Palma.bio: Honduras' most valuable player and rising star, Palma made waves in European football after joining Celtic before moving to Lech Poznan. A dynamic left winger with an exceptional left foot who has become the face of modern Honduran football.
Luis Palma.playingStyle: Explosive, skillful winger with an excellent left foot who can dribble past defenders, cut inside to shoot, or deliver pinpoint crosses
Luis Palma.worldCup2026Role: Star player and primary creative force, expected to be Honduras' most dangerous attacking weapon
Luis Palma.notableFact: Honduras' most valuable player who has played in the Scottish Premiership and Polish top flight, representing the nation's brightest talent
